name: Pasta Rosa
recipeInstructions:
1. Rehydrate the sun-dried tomatoes and cut them into julienne strips.
2. In a skillet, sauté the garlic and cayenne pepper in olive oil until fragrant.
3. Add the shrimp to the skillet and cook until they are nearly done (see note).
4. Stir in the arugula and salt. Cook just until the shrimp are tender.
5. Toss the cooked pasta with the cheese, shrimp mixture, and remaining ingredients in a serving bowl.
Yield: 4 servings.
